title: Calendar with multiple boards not showing 'overflow' items that won't fit on a calendar day
excerpt: I have a calendar view that an additional count for the number of items that can’t fit on the calendar day. When you select this, it shows the additional items. However, I also have another calendar with two board source…

posts.author: neilfry (Neil)
posts.cookedHtml:
I have a calendar view that an additional count for the number of items that can’t fit on the calendar day. When you select this, it shows the additional items. However, I also have another calendar with two board sources. This also has too many items to fit on a particular day but it neither shows you how many there are or allows you to find out what they are. The same also happens if you dock a calendar.
See pics.
Is there a way around this? Or is it a bug?


See the ‘+6 more’


The additional items are not indicated or can be viewed.

posts.author: greg_elevate (Gregory Thomson)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @neilfry - I’ve had the same issue with the calendar widget on monday.com. You basically have to resize the widget until you see the “+X more” in the corner, then you can click that as usual to see all of the items on that day. I usually just pull the corner up slightly until I see the “+X more” appear:

image1499×724 30.9 KB

Hopefully this helps!

posts.author: neilfry (Neil)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Greg. Yes, this isn’t ideal but it works. The fact that docked calendars (and hence using the most amount of screen space) can’t do this is a bit weird. This’ll do as a hack though.

posts.author: Soroh (Soroh Donn)
posts.cookedHtml: problem is though that when you adjust it - it adjusts for all users. and users with different screen sizes will view it at different times. especially an issue if you restrict dashboard editing…
